signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.895 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite US CENTCOM reporting repelled Iranian attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) represents a crowded carry trade vulnerable to geopolitical shocks. While VIX at 17.1 indicates normal volatility, further escalation toward the 160.00 level triggers BoJ intervention risk. Carry dominates the regime until VIX exceeds 25.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 spread at 1.081% as NZD outperforms peers while CAD lags. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) diverges from the RBNZ hike bias, creating short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms hawkishness. CAD remains the G8 laggard (1W avg -0.625%) as BoC 2.25% vs RBNZ 2.25% neutralizes the carry advantage. NZD/CAD strength persists as long as the bilateral spread holds above 0.80%.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7207 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 87% short. Institutional vs retail divergence is at an extreme; retail is fading the institutional long. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a 60bp carry premium supporting the AUD. Retail capitulation risk rises if price extends higher, though late-trend caution is warranted as spec longs approach the 80% extreme threshold.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.719 vs 252d norm of -0.145 (z=+1.95σ). This significant decoupling suggests EUR is trading on idiosyncratic ECB policy signals rather than regional equity flows. ECB's Schnabel notes irreversible damage from the Iran war, reinforcing a cautious hold at 2.0%. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) keeps upside structurally limited. Watch for a return to negative correlation to signal a shift back to risk-proxy trading.
